description: A deep-system cyberwarfare framework designed for realistic hacking, espionage, and digital sabotage in RPG mechanics.

Forging.purpose: Creating fake identities, websites, and deception tools
Forging.covers: Deepfake accounts, phishing, UI cloning, metadata manipulation

Infrastructure.purpose: Controlling domains, servers, and backend exploits
Infrastructure.covers: DNS spoofing, malicious hosting, email spoofing, redirection techniques

Reversing.purpose: Reverse engineering, binary patching, authentication bypassing
Reversing.covers: Cracking authentication, malware analysis, modifying software behavior

Exploiting.purpose: Injecting malware, trojans, and persistent backdoors
Exploiting.covers: Payload development, obfuscation, rootkits, trojanized documents

Interception.purpose: Sniffing, manipulating, and rerouting live system traffic
Interception.covers: Packet injection, session hijacking, log manipulation, Man-in-the-Middle attacks

Automation.purpose: Scaling attacks through botnets, AI-driven scripts, self-replicating malware
Automation.covers: Distributed hacking, self-spreading worms, mass surveillance

Exfiltration.purpose: Stealing and moving large data sets undetected
Exfiltration.covers: Covert file transfers, data compression, encrypted tunnels

Cleanup.purpose: Erasing evidence, wiping systems, forensic evasion
Cleanup.covers: Secure erase, log tampering, drive destruction, SIEM manipulation

Disruption.purpose: Destabilizing networks, DDoS attacks, targeted sabotage
Disruption.covers: DoS/DDoS attacks, cache poisoning, router exploits

Cipher (Recon).description: Scanning weaknesses, collecting data for Forging, Infrastructure, and Reversing.

Reach (Infiltration).description: Using Forging & Infrastructure to deploy phishing, fake portals, and credential harvesting.

Acquire (Privilege Escalation).description: Using Reversing & Exploiting to inject code, bypass authentication, and elevate access.

Creep (Persistence).description: Using Exploiting & Interception to deploy malware, establish backdoors, and control traffic flows.

Keyjack (Exploitation).description: Final attack phase, using Exfiltration & Automation for mass data theft, Disruption for system sabotage, Cleanup for covering tracks, and Obfuscation for long-term stealth.

design_principles.realism: Built on real-world hacking methodologies and cybersecurity techniques.
design_principles.risk_vs_reward: Players must balance speed and stealth—fast hacks raise security alerts, slow hacks risk losing the window of opportunity.
design_principles.tactical_decision_making: Each phase of hacking requires different skill applications and risk assessment.
design_principles.persistent_impact: Successful hacks permanently alter digital, corporate, and political landscapes.
